description_long: This is a large, vintage-style airplane with a deep red fuselage and wings, complemented by gray underwing and engine components. The aircraft features a straight, mid-mounted wingspan with multiple engines mounted along the leading edge, each with visible propellers. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a slightly raised canopy. The landing gear is visible and consists of multiple wheels, suggesting a robust structure for ground operations. The overall design evokes classic multi-engine propeller-driven aircraft from the early to mid-20th century.
description: A large, red and gray multi-engine propeller airplane with a straight mid-mounted wing and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, and the landing gear is robust with multiple wheels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red fuselage, gray underwing, and multiple propeller engines mounted along the wing.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, engine placement, and the symmetrical layout of the airplane.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the tail and engine arrangement from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the propeller engines, cockpit canopy, and the width of the wingspan.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Six piston engines mounted along the leading edge of the wing, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear with multiple main wheels and visible nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a raised canopy, likely multi-seat.
